title: The dispatch workflow
purpose: Put a dispatcher above the single brief: a third entry module, dispatch, takes a wave of briefed work, has the assemble_wave activity read the ledgers and cluster documents (CN1 — deterministic workflow code never touches the filesystem or resolves references), refuses briefs whose dependencies have not landed or whose coverage is broken (S4, S17), then runs stacked_dev children serially in dependency order, recording a machine-readable per-brief outcome so an AI team member can dispatch, watch, and chain waves with humans appearing only at the review gates (S1, S14, S15, S16). Serial-only is a design non-goal boundary: bounded fan-out follows once parent-close (RM-001/ADR-004) gives the dispatcher cancellation semantics over in-flight children — this brief designs no concurrency.
task: Create src/dispatch.gleam as the family's third entry module with its workflow.toml entry and dispatch_input/dispatch_output schemas, the assemble_wave dispatcher activity (definition in activities.gleam, payloads in types.gleam, codecs in codecs_workflows.gleam, implementation in locals.gleam), and the worker-side mirror (handler, serde types, SERVED_ACTIVITIES, wire pins, shim tests). DispatchInput carries a required design_dir and the wave as an ordered list of brief ids plus the shared child parameters (repo_root, base_ref, reviewers, placement, isolation, every cap and deadline) and a required halt_on_failure — nothing defaulted (ADR-001). assemble_wave resolves each brief id to its document and resolved context by reading roadmap.json, decisions.json, and the cluster documents under design_dir, orders the wave by depends_on, and refuses the whole wave when any brief is stale, coverage-broken, or dependency-blocked; the dispatch workflow then runs one stacked_dev child per entry via spawn_and_wait, strictly serially, recording landed/failed/skipped outcomes and re-registering its status query at every child transition. DEVIATION NOTES: (1) C26 reads as if the workflow input itself were the resolved wave of brief documents; per CN1/C27 the input carries brief ids plus design_dir and the documents with resolved context are produced by assemble_wave inside the run, before any workflow logic consumes them — "input order" in C28 means the assembled order, which preserves the caller's order among independent briefs. (2) The landed outcome records branch and merged_into from StackedDevResult; there is no commit hash to record because the live-proven land contract returns none and extending it is barred by CN8. (3) SPLIT on C32 (BD-005's): this brief extends wire_compat.rs with the dispatch/assemble payload pins it creates, completing the "dispatch IO" clause of that item. (4) Structure-array note: worker/src/main.rs is required here but absent from design.json's structure array — the brief is authoritative per CLAUDE.md. Out of scope: any change to stacked_dev.gleam, brief_dev.gleam, enrichment, prompts, stage contracts, concurrency or parent-close semantics, and all hermetic dispatch scenarios and e2e (BD-007).

requirements.title: Dispatch and wave types
requirements.spec: Add to src/stacked_dev/types.gleam: AssembleInput(design_dir: String, wave: List(String)); WaveEntry(brief_document: BriefDocument, resolved_context: ResolvedContext); AssembledWave(entries: List(WaveEntry)); DispatchInput(design_dir: String, wave: List(String), repo_root: String, base_ref: String, reviewers: List(String), placement: Placement, isolation: Isolation, verify_fix_cap: Int, review_cap: Int, round_backoff_ms: Int, review_deadline_ms: Int, halt_on_failure: Bool); the outcome union BriefOutcome with variants BriefLanded(brief_id: String, branch: String, merged_into: String), BriefFailed(brief_id: String, error: StackedDevError), and BriefSkipped(brief_id: String, after: String) where after names the failed brief that halted the wave; DispatchResult(outcomes: List(BriefOutcome)); the typed error DispatchError with variants AssemblyRefused(message: String) and DispatchStageFailed(stage: String, message: String); and DispatchStatus(current_brief: String, position: Int, total: Int, outcomes: List(BriefOutcome)). Every field is required — THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT give halt_on_failure, any cap, or any deadline a default (ADR-001), and SHALL NOT add a concurrency-limit field (serial-only delivery; the field arrives with the fan-out design once RM-001/ADR-004 lands).
acceptance: DispatchInput has exactly the twelve fields listed in the spec, all required by construction (Gleam records have no optional fields).
BriefOutcome has exactly three variants and BriefFailed embeds the StackedDevError type itself, not a stringified message.
No type in this change declares a field named concurrency, parallelism, or max_in_flight.

requirements.title: Hand-written dispatch codecs
requirements.spec: Add to src/stacked_dev/codecs_workflows.gleam: dispatch_input_codec(), dispatch_result_codec(), dispatch_error_codec(), dispatch_status_codec(), and codecs for AssembleInput/AssembledWave (WaveEntry reusing BD-001's brief document and resolved-context codecs). BriefOutcome encodes as a tagged object with field "outcome" taking exactly "landed", "failed", and "skipped"; the failed variant embeds the existing stacked_dev error codec's encoding under "error". These shapes contain unions, so they stay hand-written — THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT route them through aion codegen (codegen v1 rejects unions), and the decoder SHALL NOT accept an unknown outcome tag.
acceptance: Each codec round-trips a fixture: decode(encode(value)) == Ok(value) for a dispatch input, a result containing one landed, one failed, and one skipped outcome, both error variants, and a status with non-empty outcomes.
The encoded failed outcome embeds the same JSON the stacked_dev error codec produces for the same error value — one error encoding, not two.
Decoding an outcome object with "outcome":"cancelled" returns a decode error.

requirements.title: The assemble_wave activity definition
requirements.spec: Add to src/stacked_dev/activities.gleam the constant assemble_wave_name = "assemble_wave" and the typed constructor assemble_wave(input: AssembleInput) -> activity.Activity(AssembleInput, AssembledWave), wired to locals.assemble_wave with the R2 codecs, following the file's existing pattern. This is the only place ledger reading and reference resolution enter the family (CN1): the dispatch workflow body consumes AssembledWave and SHALL NOT itself read files or parse ledgers.
acceptance: activities.gleam exports assemble_wave_name with the exact value "assemble_wave" and a constructor whose activity name is that constant.
The constructor's codecs are the R2 AssembleInput/AssembledWave codecs and its local implementation is locals.assemble_wave.

requirements.title: The assemble_wave implementation: resolve, order, refuse
requirements.spec: Implement locals.assemble_wave in src/stacked_dev/locals.gleam. WHEN invoked, THE SYSTEM SHALL read <design_dir>/roadmap.json and <design_dir>/decisions.json, locate each wave brief id at <design_dir>/<cluster>/briefs/<id>.json by scanning the cluster directories (failing terminally unless exactly one match exists), decode each brief, and build its ResolvedContext: the full text (id, title, decision, and quote with its decided_by speaker, verbatim — P6) of every ADR in the brief's design_anchor from decisions.json, the texts of every C# and S# in the brief's arrays from the cluster's checklist.json and stories.json, and the provenance quote with its requested_by speaker from the roadmap item whose links.cluster equals the brief's cluster. THE SYSTEM SHALL order the returned entries so every within-wave depends_on precedes its dependent (stable topological order preserving the caller's order among independents). THE SYSTEM SHALL refuse the whole wave with a terminal failure naming every offending brief and reason — and SHALL NOT return a partial wave or silently drop a brief — when: (a) any depends_on of a wave brief is neither earlier in the wave nor recorded as landed on disk (the dependency's brief file carries an execution block with status "landed"); (b) any wave brief fails the per-brief coverage check mirrored from check-coverage.py — the brief-level checklist/stories arrays must equal the union of its per-R# arrays in both directions, and every referenced C#/S# id must exist in the cluster documents; or (c) the wave's depends_on edges contain a cycle. Refusal is a can't-execute condition (CN5): assembly never starts a doomed wave (S4, S17). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT shell out to check-coverage.py or validate.py — the checks are implemented natively so the hermetic suite's exclusive PATH can exercise them.
acceptance: Against a fixture design_dir holding two briefs where W-002 depends_on W-001 and both are in the wave as ["W-002", "W-001"], the assembled entries are ordered [W-001, W-002].

Against a fixture where the wave brief depends_on a brief that is on disk without an execution block, assemble_wave returns a terminal failure whose message contains both brief ids; with that dependency's file carrying execution.status "landed", the same call succeeds.
A fixture brief whose header checklist lists C3 while no R# cites C3 is refused with a message containing that brief's id and "C3".
A two-brief fixture whose depends_on edges form a cycle is refused with a message containing both ids.

Each assembled entry's resolved context carries the design_anchor ADR decision texts verbatim from decisions.json and the roadmap provenance quote with its requested_by speaker — string-equal to the ledger sources, never paraphrased.
grep over the assemble_wave implementation shows no invocation of python3, check-coverage.py, or validate.py.

requirements.title: The dispatch entry module
requirements.spec: Create src/dispatch.gleam with the family's entry conventions (workflow_type, definition(), run(raw) decoding the input codec, execute(input)). WHEN started, THE SYSTEM SHALL register the dispatch_status query (phase fields per DispatchStatus), run assemble_wave exactly once, and then FOR EACH assembled entry IN ORDER: re-register the status query with the entry's brief id, its 1-based position, the wave total, and the outcomes so far; build a StackedDevInput from the shared inputs plus the entry's brief_document and resolved_context with brief_id = brief_document.id; spawn the stacked_dev child via workflow.spawn_and_wait; and record BriefLanded(brief_id, branch, merged_into) on success or BriefFailed(brief_id, error) on a typed child failure. IF a child fails AND halt_on_failure is true, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL record BriefSkipped(brief_id, after: <failed id>) for every remaining entry and return the result — and SHALL NOT spawn any further child. IF a child fails AND halt_on_failure is false, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL continue to the next entry. A completed run returns DispatchResult with exactly one outcome per wave entry, in wave order: a child's typed failure is recorded data, never a dispatch failure (P3). Only can't-execute conditions are typed DispatchError: an assemble_wave failure becomes AssemblyRefused, and engine-level child errors (output/error decode failures, engine failures) become DispatchStageFailed. After the final outcome THE SYSTEM SHALL re-register the status query once more with the complete outcome list. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT spawn children concurrently and SHALL NOT retry a failed child in place — bounded fan-out is an explicit design non-goal until parent-close lands (RM-001/ADR-004), and a failed run is a terminal record whose retry is a fresh dispatch with fresh identities (ADR-005). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT read any file or resolve any reference in workflow code (CN1).
acceptance: execute contains exactly one assemble_wave activity run and exactly one spawn_and_wait call site, reached once per entry, strictly sequentially.

With a three-entry wave whose second child fails and halt_on_failure true, the result is [BriefLanded(..), BriefFailed(..), BriefSkipped(after: <second id>)] and no third child spawn occurs; with halt_on_failure false on the same wave, the third child is spawned and the result has no BriefSkipped.

The status query is registered before the first child, re-registered between every consecutive pair of child outcomes, and re-registered after the last — total registrations for an n-entry wave: n + 2.
Each child's StackedDevInput takes brief_id from that entry's brief_document.id and every cap/deadline verbatim from DispatchInput — no value is invented or altered in flight.
dispatch.gleam imports no file-IO or FFI module and contains no reference parsing — its only data sources are DispatchInput and activity/child results.

requirements.title: The dispatch manifest entry
requirements.spec: Add a [[workflow]] entry to examples/stacked-dev/workflow.toml: entry_module "dispatch", entry_function "run", input_schema "schemas/dispatch_input.json", output_schema "schemas/dispatch_output.json", activities ["assemble_wave"], output "dispatch.aion", timeout_seconds 2592000 with a comment stating the authored rationale (a multi-brief wave of children that each park up to seven days on human review; thirty days is the author's sizing of the longest legitimate wave, chosen here — not a default the engine imposes, ADR-001/ADR-003). The family deploys as a unit (CN6): the comment block SHALL note that all four archives rebuild and redeploy together.
acceptance: workflow.toml has a dispatch entry whose activities list is exactly ["assemble_wave"] and whose schema paths name the two R7 files.
timeout_seconds is 2592000 and the adjacent comment states the wave-of-review-deadlines rationale.
The existing stacked_dev, brief_dev, and gate entries are byte-unchanged by this requirement.

requirements.title: The dispatch boundary schemas
requirements.spec: Create examples/stacked-dev/schemas/dispatch_input.json and dispatch_output.json mirroring the R1 types and R2 codecs. The input schema requires exactly design_dir, wave (array of strings, minItems 1), repo_root, base_ref, reviewers (minItems 1), placement, isolation, verify_fix_cap, review_cap, round_backoff_ms, review_deadline_ms, and halt_on_failure (type boolean), with additionalProperties false and no default keyword anywhere — the caller states every value, including halt_on_failure (ADR-001). The output schema describes DispatchResult: an outcomes array of tagged objects whose "outcome" enum is exactly ["landed", "failed", "skipped"], each variant's fields required, additionalProperties false at every level.
acceptance: dispatch_input.json's required array is exactly the twelve names in the spec, root additionalProperties is false, and the string "default" appears nowhere as a schema keyword.
halt_on_failure is declared {"type": "boolean"} and is in required.

dispatch_output.json constrains outcome to the enum ["landed", "failed", "skipped"], and both files parse: `python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('examples/stacked-dev/schemas/dispatch_input.json')); json.load(open('examples/stacked-dev/schemas/dispatch_output.json'))"` exits 0.

requirements.title: Worker mirror for assemble_wave
requirements.spec: Mirror the dispatcher activity in the standalone worker: serde types for AssembleInput, WaveEntry, and AssembledWave in worker/src/types.rs (field-for-field with the R2 codecs, codec functions named in doc comments); an assemble_wave handler in worker/src/handlers.rs mirroring locals.assemble_wave decision-for-decision — same ledger reads, same resolution, same ordering, same whole-wave refusals with the same message content, refusal as a terminal ActivityFailure; and SERVED_ACTIVITIES in worker/src/main.rs grown to [&str; 12] with "assemble_wave" registered through the blocking adapter. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT implement the coverage or landed-dependency checks differently from the Gleam local — divergent refusal behaviour between local and deployed runs is a parity bug.
acceptance: SERVED_ACTIVITIES is [&str; 12] and main.rs has exactly twelve register_activity calls, the new one for "assemble_wave".

Against the same fixture design_dir, the Rust handler and the Gleam local produce an identically ordered wave for the two-brief depends_on fixture and both refuse the unlanded-dependency fixture with messages containing the same brief ids.
Every activity name listed by any [[workflow]] entry in workflow.toml (now including the dispatch entry) appears in SERVED_ACTIVITIES.

requirements.title: Wire pins and shim tests for the dispatch payloads
requirements.spec: Extend worker/tests/wire_compat.rs with byte-compatible both-direction pins for AssembleInput, WaveEntry, AssembledWave, and the dispatch IO shapes (DispatchInput, DispatchResult with all three outcome variants, DispatchError) — completing the dispatch-IO clause of C32 that BD-005 noted as split, each literal hand-derived from the R2 codec source. Extend worker/tests/handlers_shims.rs with assemble_wave coverage over fixture design_dir trees written by the tests: the ordering case, the landed-dependency acceptance, the unlanded-dependency refusal, the coverage-violation refusal, and the cycle refusal. Fixture briefs in these tests are schema-valid v2 documents — THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use invented document shapes (P4 applies to documents as much as envelopes).
acceptance: wire_compat.rs round-trips a DispatchResult literal containing one landed, one failed (embedding a stacked_dev error encoding), and one skipped outcome, byte-for-byte both directions.

handlers_shims.rs has a test per refusal class in R4's spec (unlanded dependency, coverage violation, cycle) each asserting a terminal failure naming the offending brief id, plus the ordering and landed-acceptance cases.
`cd examples/stacked-dev/worker && cargo test` passes with the new tests included.

boundaries: THE BRIEF SHALL NOT modify stacked_dev.gleam, brief_dev.gleam, gate.gleam, enrich.gleam, or prompts.gleam — dispatch composes the family, it does not reshape it (BD-002..BD-005 own those).
THE BRIEF SHALL NOT change StackedDevInput, its codec, or schemas/input.json — it consumes BD-005's shape exactly as landed.
THE BRIEF SHALL NOT modify stage schemas, stage codecs, document or resolved-context types, or the schema drift gate (BD-001).

THE BRIEF SHALL NOT implement concurrent child fan-out, a concurrency-limit input, child cancellation, or any parent-close semantics — bounded fan-out is an explicit design non-goal until parent-close (RM-001/ADR-004) lands; do not design around its absence.
THE BRIEF SHALL NOT add quorum or multi-verdict logic anywhere — review verdicts stay one signal per child, aggregated in Meridian (ADR-006).
THE BRIEF SHALL NOT add hermetic dispatch scenarios to the Gleam suite, extend the live e2e, or touch the dev-pipeline template (BD-007).
THE IMPLEMENTATION SHALL NOT retry, restart, or resume a failed child run in place — failed runs are terminal records; a new attempt is a fresh dispatch (ADR-005).
verification: Run `cd examples/stacked-dev && gleam build && gleam test` — clean, including the existing suite untouched by this brief.
Run `cd examples/stacked-dev/worker && cargo fmt --check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test` — worker gates pass with the new pins and shim tests.
Run `grep -n "@external\|simplifile\|cli\." examples/stacked-dev/src/dispatch.gleam` — no matches: the workflow body performs no IO and shells nothing (CN1).
Run `cd examples/stacked-dev && aion package . --build` (or the repo's packaging gate) and confirm four archives are reported, dispatch.aion among them.
Confirm dispatch_input.json and the DispatchInput type and codec agree on the exact twelve required fields, and that no default exists for halt_on_failure in any of the three.
Confirm BriefFailed embeds the stacked_dev error codec's encoding by comparing one literal from wire_compat.rs against the stacked_dev error pin for the same error value.
Run `grep -rn "spawn_and_wait" examples/stacked-dev/src/dispatch.gleam` — exactly one call site, inside the serial per-entry loop.
